channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senators took down one Trump Cabinet pick. But the fight over their authority is just beginning
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It's a short phrase in the Constitution, mentioned in passing. But it's already taking on an outsize significance as President-elect Donald Trump charts his return to office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Battleground Florida: Economist explains potential impact of Trump's tariffs
item.description: On WFLA's Sunday morning political show Battleground Florida, University of Tampa Economics Professor Abiail Hall Blanco explained the purpose of tariffs and how they impact families and businesses in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman admits Dems 'sort of lost ourselves' on key election issue
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman, D-Pa., said Democrats "sort of lost ourselves" on border security and immigration, resulting in the loss of Congress and the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans push back against Democrats' claims that Trump intelligence pick Gabbard is compromised
item.description: Republican lawmakers are pushing back against criticism from some Democrats that President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead U.S. intelligence services is “compromised” by her comments supportive of Russia and a meeting with an ally of that country.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: First openly trans rep-elect claims GOP spotlighting transgender issues an 'attempt to distract' voters
item.description: Delaware Democratic Rep.-elect Sarah McBride, the first openly transgender politician in the U.S. House, claimed the GOP’s focus on trans issues is an “attempt to distract" voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The rising price of paying the national debt is a risk for Trump’s promises on growth and inflation
item.description: Donald Trump has big plans for the economy. He also has big debt problem that’ll be a hurdle to delivering on those plan. Trump has bold ambitions on tax cuts, tariffs and other programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Progressives begin search for a new leader
item.description: Progressives are debating who should lead their movement as they seek to remake the Democratic Party in a new populist mold following devastating losses in November.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP motivated to rapidly confirm Trump nominations ahead of party trifecta in Washington
item.description: Republicans are signaling a quick confirmation process for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Trump’s picks for CDC, FDA and surgeon general
item.description: In a flurry of picks on Friday evening, Trump named three choices for top health jobs. Together they would help the incoming president shift the priorities of agencies that are key to public health.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Anti-abortion groups sue Illinois officials over state insurance law
item.description: The federal lawsuit was filed Wednesday by the Thomas More Society on behalf of six anti-abortion groups.
item.dc:creator: Theodora Koulouvaris, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Brooke Rollins, Trump's pick for agriculture secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has selected former White House aide Brooke Rollins to lead the Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NJ Rep. Gottheimer vows to fight congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) — Rep. Josh Gottheimer has been a staunch opponent of congestion pricing since its introduction. Now, with a new plan set in motion by New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump encounters Senate GOP limits with failed Gaetz AG push
item.description: President-elect Trump's ill-fated nomination of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to run the Justice Department marked an early sign of his potential limits in moving his nominees and dealing with Senate Republicans, giving him less wiggle room to get his Cabinet in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican who lost Minnesota House race sues claiming DFL winner lives outside district
item.description: The Roseville-area court challenge is the latest legal action Republicans are using to try and break the tie in the Minnesota House next year and muster up a majority in one chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names former Florida AG Pam Bondi as his new pick for U.S. attorney general
item.description: After former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration, President-elect Donald Trump named Pam Bondi, a former attorney general of Florida, as his next pick for U.S. attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hennepin County looks to spread the word of Minnesota’s red flag law
item.description: Nearly a year after Minnesota’s red flag law took effect, the Hennepin County Attorney’s Office says it’s looking to expand information and training on how it works.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC
